tour.welcome.title: Welcome to Stash
tour.welcome.body: Stash keeps your environment secrets encrypted, organized, and shareable. This sidebar is your command center — navigate between all the major features here.
tour.vaults.title: Your Vaults
tour.vaults.body: Each project gets its own encrypted vault. Import existing .env files by scanning your system, or create new ones from framework templates.
tour.editor.title: Editor, Diff & Team
tour.editor.body: The Editor lets you view and edit variables with validation. Diff compares profiles side-by-side. Team enables encrypted sharing with teammates.
tour.discover.title: Discover Environments
tour.discover.body: Scan your filesystem to automatically find .env files across all your projects. Import them into Stash with one click.
tour.directory.title: API Directory
tour.directory.body: Browse 600+ API services to find the keys your project needs. Search by name or filter by category, then grab keys directly from provider portals.
tour.health.title: Key Health Monitor
tour.health.body: Track staleness, expiry, format issues, and git exposure across all your projects. Catch leaked secrets before they become a problem.
tour.team.title: Team Collaboration
tour.team.body: Add teammates and share encrypted environment variables using public-key cryptography. Everyone gets the keys they need without plaintext exposure.
tour.getStarted.title: Get Started
tour.getStarted.body: Create your first encrypted environment, or scan your filesystem to import existing .env files. Your secrets are protected with AES-256-GCM encryption and Argon2id key derivation.
unlock.title: Unlock Stash
unlock.enterPassword: Enter your master password to unlock.
unlock.setPassword: Set a master password to encrypt your vault.
unlock.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters
unlock.touchIdSetupHint: Enter your password and unlock to set up Touch ID.
vaults.emptyHint: Scan your system to find .env files.
vaults.noProjects: Import a project from above to get started.
vaults.selectProjectHint: Choose a project from the sidebar to view and edit its environment variables.
vaults.deleteProjectMsg: This will remove the project from Stash. Your .env files on disk will not be deleted.
discover.emptyHint: Hit scan to search your development directories for .env files.
health.noHealthIssues: No staleness, format, or security issues detected across your projects.
health.adjustFilter: Try adjusting your search or filter.
developers.generateKeypairHint: Generate a keypair to start sharing encrypted environment variables with your team.
developers.noTeamMembersHint: Add team members above to share encrypted environment variables across your projects.
contacts.shareYourKey: Share your key so others can add you
contacts.emptyHint: Add teammates by pasting their public key, or share your invite link so others can add you.
contacts.generateFirst: Generate a keypair first to share your identity and add contacts.
contacts.pendingDesc: Someone shared their key with you. Add {{name}} to your contacts?
settings.updateAvailable: v{{version}} is available — includes improvements and fixes
settings.darkModeDesc: Toggle between light and dark theme
settings.touchIdEnabled: Enabled — unlock without typing your password
settings.touchIdDisabled: Store your vault key in the OS keychain for quick unlock
settings.cliDesc: Use Stash from the terminal with commands like stash pull, stash push, and stash switch.
settings.cliAvailable: stash is available in your terminal
envEditor.noVarsYet: No environment variables yet. Add one below, or use the New Environment wizard to generate from a template.
envVarRow.awsKeyFormat: AWS access keys should start with AKIA and be 20 characters
envVarRow.stripeKeyFormat: Stripe keys should start with sk_live_, sk_test_, pk_live_, or pk_test_
envVarRow.githubTokenFormat: GitHub tokens should start with ghp_, gho_, ghs_, or github_pat_
envVarRow.urlFormat: URL values should include a protocol (e.g. https://, postgres://)
envVarRow.portFormat: Port should be a number between 1 and 65535
envWizard.templateHint: Select a framework to pre-populate common environment variables.
envWizard.cloneHint: Clone environment variables from an existing project. Choose whether to copy values or just the key names.
envWizard.copyValuesHint: When unchecked, only key names are copied — you'll fill in values yourself.
profileSwitcher.createBasedOn: Create a new profile based on "{{profile}}"
diffView.hint: Compare environment variables across profiles to spot differences between dev, staging, and production.
diffView.selectDifferent: Select two different profiles to compare.
gitBanner.tracked: .env is tracked in git — secrets may be exposed in commit history
gitBanner.notIgnored: .env is not in .gitignore — it could be accidentally committed
teamPanel.step1: Each team member generates a keypair — your private key never leaves your machine
teamPanel.step2: Share public keys with each other and add them as team members
teamPanel.step3: Push encrypts your .env values for each member and writes to .stash.lock
teamPanel.step4: .stash.lock is safe to commit to git — values are encrypted per-member
teamPanel.step5: Pull decrypts the values meant for you using your private key
teamPanel.shareKeyHint: Share this public key with your teammates so they can add you.
teamPanel.generateKeypairHint: Generate a keypair to start sharing encrypted environment variables with your team. Your private key stays on this machine and is never shared.
teamPanel.syncHint: After pushing, commit .stash.lock to git. Teammates pull after checking out your changes.
teamPanel.noMembersHint: No team members yet. Share your public key with teammates and ask for theirs. Once added, Push will encrypt your .env values for each member.
teamPanel.pushSuccess: Encrypted and written to .stash.lock — you can now commit this file to git
guide.vaults.title: How Vaults Work
guide.vaults.step1: Each project gets its own encrypted vault, secured with your master password
guide.vaults.step2: Import existing .env files from disk or create new ones from framework templates
guide.vaults.step3: Profiles let you manage dev, staging, and production configs side by side
guide.vaults.step4: All data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM and your key is derived using Argon2id
guide.discover.title: How Scanning Works
guide.discover.step1: Stash scans common development directories for .env files
guide.discover.step2: Detected projects show their framework, path, and number of env files
guide.discover.step3: Import a project to add it to your encrypted vault with one click
guide.discover.step4: Already-imported projects can be opened directly in the Vaults view
guide.health.title: What Health Checks For
guide.health.step1: Stale keys — flags variables that haven't been rotated in 30+ or 90+ days
guide.health.step2: Format validation — checks AWS keys, Stripe keys, GitHub tokens, URLs, and ports
guide.health.step3: Git exposure — scans your repos to find .env files tracked in version control
guide.health.step4: Overlaps — detects the same key name used across multiple projects
guide.health.step5: Expiring keys — warns you before key expiration dates you've set
guide.diff.title: How Profile Diff Works
guide.diff.step1: Select two profiles (e.g. dev and production) to compare side by side
guide.diff.step2: Added, removed, and changed variables are highlighted automatically
guide.diff.step3: Identical variables are collapsed but can be expanded
cli.bannerDesc: Install the CLI to pull, push, and switch environments directly from the command line.
savedKeys.title: Saved Keys
savedKeys.emptyHint: Save API keys from the Directory or import them from the browser extension.
guide.editor.title: Environment Editor Tips
guide.editor.step1: Click the dots to reveal a value, or use the eye icon to toggle visibility
guide.editor.step2: Values are validated in real-time — warnings appear for malformed keys
guide.editor.step3: Use the clock icon to view change history and restore previous values
guide.editor.step4: Set expiration dates on keys with the calendar icon to track rotation
